_note: Long-tail macro data. Context for equity/bond market positioning.

us_dollar_index_dxy.impact: Strong dollar = headwind for US multinational earnings

gold_price.note: All-time high; geopolitical safe haven + de-dollarization

yield_curve_interpretation.normal: Long rates > short rates; growth expected
yield_curve_interpretation.flat: Rates equal across maturities; transition period
yield_curve_interpretation.inverted: Short > long; historically predicts recession within 12-18 months
yield_curve_interpretation.historical_accuracy: Yield curve inverted before every recession since 1970
yield_curve_interpretation.2s10s_spread_significance: Most watched inversion signal; -50bps or below = strong recession warning
yield_curve_interpretation.current_2026_reading: Steepening again; Fed cut cycle underway; watch for normalization

fed_policy_framework.forward_guidance: Fed signals future intentions; markets price in before action
fed_policy_framework.quantitative_tightening_qt: Fed reduces balance sheet by not reinvesting maturing bonds
fed_policy_framework.dot_plot: Quarterly FOMC median rate projections; market signal for rate path

ism_manufacturing_pmi.note: Below 45 for 3+ months often precedes recession

leading_indicators.housing_starts: Leads economy by 6-12 months; sensitive to mortgage rates

high_yield_vs_treasury.note: Wide spreads = credit stress; narrow = risk appetite

leading_indicators.consumer_confidence: Michigan Sentiment + Conference Board; leads consumer spending 2-3 months
leading_indicators.initial_jobless_claims: Weekly leading indicator; >300K 4-week avg = labor market weakening

global_macro_factors.geopolitical_risk_premium: Russian Ukraine ongoing; Middle East conflict; Taiwan strait tension adds ~5% oil premium
global_macro_factors.note_on_deficit: High deficit in non-recession environment is unusual; bond vigilantes could push long rates higher
